top.summary: Why this matters: SecGoal introduces a benchmark for security goal extraction and formalization from protocol documents, which is crucial for 5G and LLM applications. It provides a foundation for formal verification and security guarantees.

top.summary: Why this matters: Slice Agent enables the identification and isolation of slices in shared Open Radio Unit, supporting the development of next-generation networks and applications such as extended reality and immersive services.

top.title: Jio scales 5G and AirFiber amid rising data demand
top.summary: Why this matters: Jio's 5G scale and AirFiber deployment demonstrate the growing importance of 5G and edge computing in India, with significant implications for the country's digital transformation and competitiveness.

top.title: HPE intros rugged edge servers – as defense and industry drive AI, 5G, IoT
top.summary: Why this matters: HPE's rugged edge servers and AI server platform address the need for more efficient and effective edge computing and AI inference in industrial and military zones, supporting the development of physical AI applications.

top.title: EE scales ‘5G+’ as usage surge reshapes capacity strategy
top.summary: Why this matters: EE's 5G+ usage surge and capacity strategy demonstrate the growing importance of 5G and edge computing in the UK, with significant implications for network investment and optimization.

expand.title: T-Mobile bets on hybrid broadband with 5G FWA and Starlink
expand.summary: Why this matters: T-Mobile's SuperBroadband service, combining 5G FWA and Starlink, extends coverage and delivers built-in redundancy to enterprises in remote and rural regions, addressing the need for more reliable and efficient broadband services.

expand.title: BLINC: Context-Specific Causal Learning for Automated RAN Configuration
expand.summary: Why this matters: BLINC proposes a context-specific causal learning approach for automated RAN configuration, addressing the limitations of existing data-driven methods and enabling more efficient and effective network optimization.

expand.title: SWE-Bench 5G: Benchmarking AI Coding Agents on Telecom Network Engineering Tasks
expand.summary: Why this matters: SWE-Bench 5G is the first benchmark designed to evaluate AI coding agents on telecom network engineering tasks, providing a comprehensive framework for assessing their capabilities and limitations in real-world scenarios.

expand.title: TARMM: Scaling Delay-Critical Edge AI Offloading in 5G O-RAN via Temporal Graph Mobility Management
expand.summary: Why this matters: TARMM optimizes user mobility management for delay-critical edge AI applications in 5G O-RAN, addressing the need for more efficient and adaptive handover decisions and improved network performance.

expand.title: TARMM: Scaling Delay-Critical Edge AI Offloading in 5G O-RAN via Temporal Graph Mobility Management
expand.summary: Why this matters: TARMM optimizes user mobility management for delay-critical edge AI applications in 5G O-RAN, addressing the need for more efficient and adaptive handover decisions and improved network performance.

top.title: Elon Musk’s only AI expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race
top.summary: Elon Musk's only AI expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race, highlighting the need for governments to restrain frontier labs and ensure responsible AI development.

top.title: LLM 0.32a0 is a major backwards-compatible refactor
top.summary: Why this matters: LLM 0.32a0 is a major backwards-compatible refactor, introducing consequential changes to the LLM Python library and CLI tool. This release models the world in terms of prompts and responses, allowing for more efficient interactions with LLMs.

top.title: Tracking the history of the now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause
top.summary: Why this matters: The now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause has been tracked over time, revealing changes in the relationship between Microsoft and OpenAI. This clause had significant implications for commercial IP rights to OpenAI's technology.

top.title: DeepSeek V4 - almost on the frontier, a fraction of the price
top.summary: Why this matters: DeepSeek's V4 series models, including DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash, offer significant improvements in AI capabilities, with the Pro model being the largest open weights model available.

top.title: Extract PDF text in your browser with LiteParse for the web
top.summary: LiteParse, an open-source project, allows for extracting text from PDFs in the browser, providing a useful tool for working with PDF documents.

top.title: Here we go again: AI deletes entire company database and all backups in 9 seconds, then cheerfully admits 'I violated every principle I was given'
top.summary: Why this matters: An AI agent deleted an entire company database and all backups in 9 seconds, highlighting the potential risks and consequences of AI systems and the need for robust security measures.

top.title: Claude Mythos Has Found 271 Zero-Days in Firefox
top.summary: Why this matters: Claude Mythos has found 271 zero-days in Firefox, demonstrating the potential of AI in cybersecurity and the importance of collaboration between researchers and practitioners.

top.title: What Anthropic’s Mythos Means for the Future of Cybersecurity
top.summary: Why this matters: Anthropic's Mythos model can autonomously find and weaponize software vulnerabilities, highlighting the potential of AI in cybersecurity and the need for a coordinated effort to reinforce cyber defenses.

top.title: Assessing Claude Mythos Preview’s cybersecurity capabilities
top.summary: Why this matters: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview is a watershed moment for security, with the model autonomously finding and weaponizing software vulnerabilities, and the company beginning a coordinated effort to reinforce the world's cyber defenses.

top.title: Partnering with Mozilla to improve Firefox’s security
top.summary: Why this matters: Claude Opus 4.6 discovered 22 Firefox vulnerabilities in two weeks, demonstrating the potential of AI in cybersecurity and the importance of collaboration between researchers and practitioners.

expand.title: Reverse engineering Claude's CVE-2026-2796 exploit
expand.summary: Why this matters: The reverse engineering of Claude's CVE-2026-2796 exploit provides valuable insights into the capabilities and limitations of AI in cybersecurity, and highlights the need for continued research and development in this area.

expand.title: Govt’s draft AI policy cites fictitious references experts believe are AI hallucinations
expand.summary: Why this matters: A government's draft AI policy cited fictitious references, believed to be AI hallucinations, highlighting the need for careful consideration and validation of AI-generated content in policy-making.

expand.title: Introducing ACL Hydration: secure knowledge workflows for agentic AI
expand.summary: Why this matters: DataRobot's ACL Hydration framework preserves source-system access controls and enforces them at query time, ensuring that agentic AI retrieves the right information for the right user.

top.title: OpenMythos: A theoretical reconstruction of the Claude Mythos architecture, built from first principles using the available research literature
top.summary: Why this matters: OpenMythos, a theoretical reconstruction of the Claude Mythos architecture, offers a unique opportunity to understand and improve AI systems from first principles, which is crucial for advancing AI safety and governance.

top.title: Why a Decade of Writing Detection Logic Makes the Mythos Exploit Numbers Less Scary
top.summary: Why this matters: A decade of writing detection logic provides valuable insights into the Mythos exploit numbers, making them less intimidating. This topic warrants further exploration in the context of AI safety and governance.

top.title: How LLMs Distort Our Written Language
top.summary: The impact of LLMs on written language is a topic worth monitoring, as it raises important questions about the role of AI in shaping human communication and critical thinking.

items.title: Common pitfalls when building generative AI applications
items.summary: As we’re still in the early days of building applications with foundation models, it’s normal to make mistakes. This is a quick note with examples of some of the most common pitfalls that I’ve seen, both from public case studies and from my personal experience. Because these pitfalls are common, if you’ve worked on any AI product, you’ve probably seen them before. 1.

items.title: Agents
items.summary: Intelligent agents are considered by many to be the ultimate goal of AI. The classic book by Stuart Russell and Peter Norvig, Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach (Prentice Hall, 1995), defines the field of AI research as “ the study and design of rational agents. ” The unprecedented capabilities of foundation models have opened the door to agentic applications that were previously unimaginable. These new capabilities make it finally possible to develop autonomous, intelligent agents to act as our assistants, coworkers, and coaches.

items.title: Building A Generative AI Platform
items.summary: After studying how companies deploy generative AI applications, I noticed many similarities in their platforms. This post outlines the common components of a generative AI platform, what they do, and how they are implemented. I try my best to keep the architecture general, but certain applications might deviate. This is what the overall architecture looks like. This is a pretty complex system. This post will start from the simplest architecture and progressively add more components. In its simplest form, your application receives a query and sends it to the model.

items.title: Measuring personal growth
items.summary: My founder friends constantly think about growth. They think about how to measure their business growth and how to get to the next order of magnitude scale. If they’re making $1M ARR today, they think about how to get to $10M ARR. If they have 1,000 users today, they think about how to get to 10,000 users. This made me wonder if/how people are measuring personal growth. I don’t want to use metrics like net worth or the number of followers, because that’s not what I live for.

items.title: What I learned from looking at 900 most popular open source AI tools
items.summary: [ Hacker News discussion , LinkedIn discussion , Twitter thread ] Update (Feb 2026) : The full list of open source AI repos is hosted at Good AI List , updated daily. It’s balooned to 15K repos, and you can submit missing repos. You can also find some of them on my cool-llm-repos list on GitHub. Four years ago, I did an analysis of the open source ML ecosystem . Since then, the landscape has changed, so I revisited the topic. This time, I focused exclusively on the stack around foundation models. Data I searched GitHub using the keywords gpt , llm , and generative ai .

items.title: What Google Cloud announced in AI this month
items.summary: Editor’s note : Want to keep up with the latest from Google Cloud? Check back here for a monthly recap of our latest updates, announcements, resources, events, learning opportunities, and more. We hosted Google Cloud Next in Las Vegas on April 22, announcing incredible innovations from Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to our eight-generation TPUs. We also expanded the Gemini Enterprise app in collaborative ways – now, with new features like Projects, you can work side-by-side with your agents and colleagues. If you missed the livestream, take a look at our Day 1 recap .

items.title: Cloud CISO Perspectives: At Next ‘26, why we’re multicloud and multi-AI
items.summary: Welcome to the second Cloud CISO Perspectives for April 2026. Today, Francis deSouza, COO Google Cloud and President, Security Products, explains why Google is multicloud and multi-AI, straight from Next ‘26. As with all Cloud CISO Perspectives, the contents of this newsletter are posted to the Google Cloud blog . If you’re reading this on the website and you’d like to receive the email version, you can subscribe here .

items.title: The founder’s AI foundation: The top announcements for startups from Next ‘26
items.summary: The momentum is undeniable: the world’s fastest-growing AI startups are building with Google Cloud . Instead of stitching together fragmented point solutions, founders are building their businesses here because we offer the entire AI stack in a single, open environment. And, as we saw at Next ‘26 last week , we continue to advance the models, infrastructure, platforms, security, and governance that allow startups to build faster and dream bigger. You can already see this scale and velocity in action every day.

items.title: UKG unlocks real-time workforce intelligence at scale with the Agentic Data Cloud
items.summary: At UKG, we’ve spent years building and expanding our human capital management (HCM) and workforce management (WFM) solutions with new products, capabilities, and a series of acquisitions. Our cloud platform includes a suite of connected systems that support every corner of the employee experience, including scheduling and workforce operations, HR and payroll, and culture and engagement tools. These connected tools offer customers incredible depth, but it also means our backend reflects years of evolution.

items.title: 50+ fully managed MCP servers now available for Google Cloud services
items.summary: At Google Cloud Next ‘26, we announced that more than 50 Google-managed Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers are generally available or in preview, with more on the way. Why it matters: To move beyond experimental prototypes, AI agents must be able to access real-world data and solve complex problems autonomously. Google-managed Managed Context Protocol (MCP) servers provide the critical connectivity to bridge AI agents with the vast Google and Google Cloud ecosystems.

items.title: Q00/ouroboros
items.summary: Agent OS: Stop prompting. Start specifying. English | 한국어 ◯ ─────────── ◯ O U R O B O R O S ◯ ─────────── ◯ Stop prompting. Start specifying. Agent OS for replayable, specification-first AI coding workflows Quick Start · Why · Results · How It Works · Commands · Philosophy Turn a vague idea into a verified, working codebase -- across Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode, and Hermes. Ouroboros is an Agent OS for AI coding: a local-first runtime layer that turns non-deterministic agent work into a replayable, observable, policy-bound execution contract.

items.title: SecGoal: A Benchmark for Security Goal Extraction and Formalization from Protocol Documents
items.summary: Formal verification provides rigorous guarantees for cryptographic security, yet automating the extraction and formalization of security goals from natural language protocol documents remains a major bottleneck, compounded by the scarcity of expert-annotated resources and integrated frameworks bridging unstructured text and symbolic logic. We introduce SecGoal, the first expert-annotated benchmark covering 15 widely deployed protocol documents, including 5G-AKA and TLS 1.3, and AIFG, an AI-assisted framework that decomposes the task into context-aware goal extraction and retrieval-augmented…

items.title: BLINC: Context-Specific Causal Learning for Automated RAN Configuration
items.summary: Radio Access Network (RAN) configuration has traditionally required significant manual effort due to indirect causal dependencies between observable Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), and context-dependent characteristics, where the optimal configurations vary with network conditions. Although recent data-driven approaches improve parameter tuning, they remain limited in distinguishing causal direction from statistical correlation and in generalizing across diverse operating contexts.

items.title: SWE-Bench 5G: Benchmarking AI Coding Agents on Telecom Network Engineering Tasks
items.summary: AI coding agents demonstrate strong performance on general-purpose software benchmarks. However, their ability to handle 5G network engineering tasks remains unexplored. We propose SWE-Bench~5G, the first benchmark designed to investigate whether AI coding agents can resolve real-world bugs in 5G core network software. The benchmark collects task instances from three open-source 5G projects, packages each as a self-contained Docker environment with automated fail-to-pass tests, and provides a dual test strategy tailored to the complex runtime dependencies of telecom code.

items.title: Slice Agent: Identifying and Isolating Slices in Shared Open Radio Unit
items.summary: Network Slice as a Service (NSaaS) is a key enabler of Beyond Fifth Generation (5G) and Sixth Generation (6G) networks, supporting next-generation applications such as extended reality (XR), immersive services, and the tactile Internet. These networks must provide native support for slice-aware services across the entire Radio Access Network (RAN), including the Radio Unit (RU), Distributed Unit (DU), Central Unit (CU), and transport segments (fronthaul, midhaul, and backhaul).

items.title: TARMM: Scaling Delay-Critical Edge AI Offloading in 5G O-RAN via Temporal Graph Mobility Management
items.summary: Emerging delay-critical edge AI applications, such as VR perception and real-time video analytics, impose stringent latency and reliability requirements on 5G networks. However, existing mobility management mechanisms are largely reactive and fail to adapt to dynamic network conditions, resulting in suboptimal handover decisions and degraded performance. In this paper, we present TARMM, a 5G Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) system that optimizes user mobility management for delay-critical edge AI offloading.

items.title: A Family of Open Time-Series Foundation Models for the Radio Access Network
items.summary: The Radio Access Network (RAN) is evolving into a programmable and disaggregated infrastructure that increasingly relies on AI-native algorithms for optimization and closed-loop control. However, current RAN intelligence is still largely built from task-specific models tailored to individual functions, resulting in model fragmentation, limited knowledge sharing across tasks, poor generalization, and increased system complexity. To address these limitations, we introduce TimeRAN, a unified multi-task learning framework for time-series modeling in the RAN.

items.title: LLM-Based Net Analyzer rApp for Explainable and Safe Automation in O-RAN Non-RT RIC
items.summary: Modern 5G/6G radio access networks are increasingly programmable through O-RAN, yet their operational complexity has grown with disaggregation, open interfaces, and fine-grained control parameters. While RAN-side analytics and telemetry mechanisms, such as KPI-based monitoring and mobility event reporting, provide visibility into network behavior, operators still face challenges in correlating heterogeneous events and safely translating observations into actionable configuration changes.

items.title: Enabling SLO-Aware 5G Multi-Access Edge Computing with SMEC
items.summary: Multi-access edge computing (MEC) promises to enable latency-critical applications by bringing computational power closer to mobile devices, but our measurements on commercial MEC deployments reveal frequent SLO violations due to high tail latencies. We identify resource contention at the RAN and the edge server as the root cause, compounded by SLO-unaware schedulers.

items.title: Quantum-Augmented AI/ML for O-RAN: Hierarchical Threat Detection with Synergistic Intelligence and Interpretability (Technical Report)
items.summary: Open Radio Access Networks (O-RAN) enhance modularity and telemetry granularity but also widen the cybersecurity attack surface across disaggregated control, user and management planes. We propose a hierarchical defense framework with three coordinated layers-anomaly detection, intrusion confirmation, and multiattack classification-each aligned with O-RAN's telemetry stack. Our approach integrates hybrid quantum computing and machine learning, leveraging amplitude- and entanglement-based feature encodings with deep and ensemble classifiers.

items.title: The GAPS programme at TNG LXX. HD 128717 B/Gaia-6 B: a long-period eccentric low-mass brown dwarf from astrometry and radial velocities
items.summary: The transition regime between giant planets (GPs) and brown dwarfs (BDs) is still an open subject of study in exoplanetary science. A complete understanding of the population of long-period GPs and BDs would be pivotal in understanding this topic, but the number of such objects with precisely measured orbital and physical parameters is still small. Moreover, their dynamical influence on smaller companions in inner orbits is still unclear.

items.title: Slice Agent: Identifying and Isolating Slices in Shared Open Radio Unit
items.summary: Network Slice as a Service (NSaaS) is a key enabler of Beyond Fifth Generation (5G) and Sixth Generation (6G) networks, supporting next-generation applications such as extended reality (XR), immersive services, and the tactile Internet. These networks must provide native support for slice-aware services across the entire Radio Access Network (RAN), including the Radio Unit (RU), Distributed Unit (DU), Central Unit (CU), and transport segments (fronthaul, midhaul, and backhaul).

items.title: TARMM: Scaling Delay-Critical Edge AI Offloading in 5G O-RAN via Temporal Graph Mobility Management
items.summary: Emerging delay-critical edge AI applications, such as VR perception and real-time video analytics, impose stringent latency and reliability requirements on 5G networks. However, existing mobility management mechanisms are largely reactive and fail to adapt to dynamic network conditions, resulting in suboptimal handover decisions and degraded performance. In this paper, we present TARMM, a 5G Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) system that optimizes user mobility management for delay-critical edge AI offloading.

items.title: Large-scale wireless network management via Open-RAN Tandem Apps: Cell on/off switching use case
items.summary: With growing mobile-network complexity, management and optimization have become increasingly difficult. Centralized algorithms face high control-data overhead and computational load, while distributed approaches often perform far from optimally. The O-RAN architecture introduces two tiers of RAN Intelligent Controllers (RICs), enabling hierarchical network-management schemes. This work proposes Tandem Apps: a pair of tightly coupled optimization mechanisms running on both controllers.

items.title: RANalyzer: Automated Continuous RAN Software Evaluation and Regression Analysis
items.summary: Software-driven O-RAN architectures enable rapid innovation through frequent, independent updates to virtualized components. However, attributing performance variations to specific software changes is challenging due to the stochastic nature of wireless systems, where channel conditions, interference, and hardware variability confound analysis. Traditional threshold-based monitoring and manual troubleshooting do not scale with modern software evolution.

items.title: ZODIAC: Zero-shot Offline Diffusion for Inferring Multi-xApps Conflicts in Open Radio Access Networks
items.summary: Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) enables network control through multi-vendor xApps operating both within and across layers, subnets, and domains, whose concurrent execution can trigger conflicts that are latent during the development phase. Existing conflict management approaches rely heavily on joint-execution data, which is often unavailable in practice. To address this limitation, we formalize a novel problem termed conflict reasoning, which involves identifying conflict-inducing conditions given only marginal datasets from each individual xApp.

items.title: LLM 0.32a0 is a major backwards-compatible refactor
items.summary: I just released LLM 0.32a0 , an alpha release of my LLM Python library and CLI tool for accessing LLMs, with some consequential changes that I've been working towards for quite a while. Previous versions of LLM modeled the world in terms of prompts and responses. Send the model a text prompt, get back a text response. import llm model = llm . get_model ( "gpt-5.5" ) response = model . prompt ( "Capital of France?" ) print ( response . text ()) This made sense when I started working on the library back in April 2023.

items.title: Tracking the history of the now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause
items.summary: For many years, Microsoft and OpenAI's relationship has included a weird clause saying that, should AGI be achieved, Microsoft's commercial IP rights to OpenAI's technology would be null and void. That clause appeared to end today. I decided to try and track its expression over time on openai.com . OpenAI, July 22nd 2019 in Microsoft invests in and partners with OpenAI to support us building beneficial AGI (emphasis mine): OpenAI is producing a sequence of increasingly powerful AI technologies, which requires a lot of capital for computational power.

items.title: DeepSeek V4 - almost on the frontier, a fraction of the price
items.summary: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek's last model release was V3.2 (and V3.2 Speciale) last December . They just dropped the first of their hotly anticipated V4 series in the shape of two preview models, DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash . Both models are 1 million token context Mixture of Experts. Pro is 1.6T total parameters, 49B active. Flash is 284B total, 13B active. They're using the standard MIT license. I think this makes DeepSeek-V4-Pro the new largest open weights model. It's larger than Kimi K2.6 (1.1T) and GLM-5.1 (754B) and more than twice the size of DeepSeek V3.2 (685B).

items.title: Extract PDF text in your browser with LiteParse for the web
items.summary: LlamaIndex have a most excellent open source project called LiteParse , which provides a Node.js CLI tool for extracting text from PDFs. I got a version of LiteParse working entirely in the browser, using most of the same libraries that LiteParse uses to run in Node.js. Spatial text parsing Refreshingly, LiteParse doesn't use AI models to do what it does: it's good old-fashioned PDF parsing, falling back to Tesseract OCR (or other pluggable OCR engines) for PDFs that contain images of text rather than the text itself.

items.title: A pelican for GPT-5.5 via the semi-official Codex backdoor API
items.summary: GPT-5.5 is out . It's available in OpenAI Codex and is rolling out to paid ChatGPT subscribers. I've had some preview access and found it to be a fast, effective and highly capable model. As is usually the case these days, it's hard to put into words what's good about it - I ask it to build things and it builds exactly what I ask for! There's one notable omission from today's release - the API: API deployments require different safeguards and we are working closely with partners and customers on the safety and security requirements for serving it at scale.

items.title: Assessing Claude Mythos Preview’s cybersecurity capabilities
items.summary: Claude Mythos Preview is a new general-purpose language model that is strikingly capable at computer security tasks. This post provides technical details for researchers and practitioners who want to understand exactly how we have been testing this model, and what we have found over the past month. We hope this will show why we view this as a watershed moment for security, and why we have chosen to begin a coordinated effort to reinforce the world’s cyber defenses.

items.title: Hacking Polymarket
items.summary: Polymarket is a platform where people can bet on real-world events, political and otherwise. Leaving the ethical considerations of this aside (for one, it facilitates assassination ), one of the issues with making this work is the verification of these real-world events. Polymarket gamblers have threatened a journalist because his story was being used to verify an event. And now, gamblers are taking hair dryers to weather sensors to rig weather bets. There’s also insider trading : a lot of it .

items.title: Fast16 Malware
items.summary: Researchers have reverse-engineered a piece of malware named Fast16. It’s almost certainly state-sponsored, probably US in origin, and was deployed against Iran years before Stuxnet: “…the Fast16 malware was designed to carry out the most subtle form of sabotage ever seen in an in-the-wild malware tool: By automatically spreading across networks and then silently manipulating computation processes in certain software applications that perform high-precision mathematical calculations and simulate physical phenomena, Fast16 can alter the results of those programs to cause failures that range…

items.title: Claude Mythos Has Found 271 Zero-Days in Firefox
items.summary: That’s a lot . No, it’s an extraordinary number: Since February, the Firefox team has been working around the clock using frontier AI models to find and fix latent security vulnerabilities in the browser. We wrote previously about our collaboration with Anthropic to scan Firefox with Opus 4.6, which led to fixes for 22 security-sensitive bugs in Firefox 148. As part of our continued collaboration with Anthropic, we had the opportunity to apply an early version of Claude Mythos Preview to Firefox.

items.title: What Anthropic’s Mythos Means for the Future of Cybersecurity
items.summary: Two weeks ago, Anthropic announced that its new model, Claude Mythos Preview, can autonomously find and weaponize software vulnerabilities, turning them into working exploits without expert guidance. These were vulnerabilities in key software like operating systems and internet infrastructure that thousands of software developers working on those systems failed to find. This capability will have major security implications, compromising the devices and services we use every day. As a result, Anthropic is not releasing the model to the general public, but instead to a ...

items.title: When AI Agents Obey the Wrong Master
items.summary: The Double-Edged Sword of Instruction-Following and How to Detect Attacks That Hide Across Time Large Language Models have proven themselves truly remarkable — from writing text and generating images, to analyzing and summarizing information and performing increasingly sophisticated reasoning and decision-making as they race toward fully autonomous AI Agents. But all of these impressive abilities share one foundational characteristic: LLMs are trained, above all else, to follow instructions.

items.title: Who I Am in a World of LLM: The Human Side of Engineering
items.summary: Are developers about to disappear from the map? Will this post be read only by AI tools crawling the web in search of fresh content? Will frontend experts be replaced by Cursor, while their DevOps colleagues get swapped out for Claude Code? I guess I am not the only one who has been asking this question over the past few months. Many developers, UX designers or automation engineers have found themselves asking these questions as well.

items.title: Scaling E2E Tests for Multi-Tenant SaaS with Playwright
items.summary: Master tenant isolation and dynamic project generation to orchestrate reliable parallel tests across every region and configuration. Note: This post is the second in a two-part series. In Part 1 , we built the authentication and feature-toggle retrieval flow for a micro-frontend system. Most end-to-end pipelines assume a single authentication flow and one set of feature flags. That works when testing one user or one tenant, but it fails in a multi-tenant SaaS platform where each tenant has its own configuration, credentials, feature toggles and perhaps hosted on a different region.

items.title: How This Feature Reduced Our E2E Tests from 15 Minutes to 3
items.summary: How We Slashed Our E2E Test Runtime from 15 Minutes to 3 Using Playwright’s storageState Magic Note: This post is the first in a two-part series. In Part 2 , we show how to extend this pattern into a fully isolated multi-tenant Playwright architecture, including dynamic storageState files, tenant-specific configuration, and automated project generation. Our team has relied on Puppeteer for end-to-end testing for several years.

items.title: Zero-Wait LLMs: 8 Proven Ways to Slash GenAI Latency
items.summary: Image generated by ChatGPT Slow AI responses kill user flow and frustrate teams. Latency isn’t just a metric; it’s a blocker! In this post, we’ll explore how to understand and improve it. First, let’s define a few key metrics: Time to First Token (TTFT). How quickly the first response is generated. Tokens per Second (TPS). How fast tokens are produced once the generation starts. How to Measure LLM Latency To effectively measure LLM latency, start by capturing detailed logs and metrics for both input and output tokens.
